claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch uses provider-adapter to configure Claude agents with boundary-controlled tools through CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS and DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS, relies on post-execution boundary snapshots to detect project mutations, and implements provider-specific isolation but defaults Claude to full tool availability pending runtime stress verification.

evidence.summary: CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS constant defines allowed tools including Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, NotebookEdit, Bash, Glob, Grep, WebFetch, WebSearch, Agent, TaskCreate, TaskUpdate
evidence.why: This is the tool allowlist that evidence agents receive by default

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS defines permissions.allow as CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS array
evidence.why: This shows the boundary schema applied to Claude agent settings

evidence.summary: executionConfig normalizes agentToolProfile to 'research', 'full', or 'boundary' and applies DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS for Claude provider
evidence.why: This is where provider-specific configuration happens and boundary settings are applied

evidence.summary: Runtime builds boundary snapshot before agent execution, runs agents, then builds after snapshot and diffs to detect mutations
evidence.why: This is the core boundary enforcement mechanism - post-execution audit not pre-blocking

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation for Claude constructs --allowedTools, --disallowedTools, --permission-mode, --settings flags from config
evidence.why: This translates boundary settings into Claude CLI arguments

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceTasks creates explorer, cross-checker, verifier tasks based on evidence_policy
evidence.why: This defines the evidence agent workflow

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues converts missing, clarify, block recommendations into blockers
evidence.why: This is how evidence agent results block PRD readiness

evidence.summary: Test verifies boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass
evidence.why: This confirms boundary enforcement is implemented and tested

evidence.summary: Test verifies codex dispatch does not inherit claude default model and allows unset sandbox defaults
evidence.why: This confirms provider-specific isolation is implemented

evidence.summary: Test confirms execute mode requires both executeAgents and allowAgentDispatch authorization
evidence.why: This verifies the dual-gate execution policy

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch shows evidence_workflow=blocked with missing EVIDENCE_REQUIRED blocker
evidence.why: This shows current state and that evidence workflow is not passing PRD readiness gates

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_SETTINGS does NOT define deny list - Write, Edit, Bash tools are allowed by default, not pre-blocked
evidence.why: This reveals boundary relies on post-execution audit, not pre-execution tool denial

assumptions: CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS list is current and complete for Claude Code capabilities
Provider configuration preserves full tool capability under harness boundary (not yet runtime-verified with real Claude -p agents)
Cross-provider model leakage does not occur in current implementation (assumed from provider isolation code)
Post-execution boundary audit is sufficient to prevent target-project corruption (assumes no false negatives in snapshot diff)
risks: A provider-specific assumption can silently weaken dispatch or break real agent calls
If boundary snapshot misses mutation types or has false negatives, project files can be corrupted without blocker
CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS may be incomplete or outdated relative to Claude Code's actual tool set
Default full tool availability may not match actual harness boundary behavior in production Claude Code CLI
missing: Runtime verification with real Claude -p agents that boundary settings actually restrict tool usage as configured
Integration test coverage for provider isolation preventing cross-provider model leakage
Evidence that CLAUDE_BOUNDARY_TOOLS matches Claude Code's current available tool set
Verification that post-execution boundary audit catches all mutation types (symlinks, permissions, metadata, etc.)

result.notes: Source code evidence confirms implementation of boundary-controlled dispatch through provider-adapter and post-execution snapshot auditing. Provider-specific isolation exists in code and tests. However, the current demand itself requires cross_check evidence policy due to EXISTING_PROJECT_FACTS and TECHNICAL_CONTRACT_OR_DATA_RISK triggers, and acceptance criteria explicitly require verification before PRD readiness. Key gaps remain between code-defined boundary settings and runtime-verified behavior with real agents.
